Keen Appraisal would name it better than Resonant Sight can at this range and through this medium, but what Resonant Sight gives you is shape: a chamber, underground, beneath the forward alcove's diagonal, accessible from the shaped corridor's intersection point. The same chamber you have been speaking with. Just — below you, not in front of you.

Teva feels something shift through the earth under her palms. She lifts her chin. 'The bearing changed,' she says quietly. 'Forty paces, going down.' Aevi stays locked northeast-by-east at the berm-crest, hard-white and silent, every mote contracted to a point — but one mote at the outer edge pulses once, so faintly you might have imagined it.

turns.player: Ask Aevi plainly what it recognized at the shimmer crossing — it has been silent long enough; name that silence and hold a space for it
turns.summary: Aevi names what it recognized at the shimmer — a shaped boundary condition, built by someone, and the recognition was involuntary, older than its own memory.
turns.narration:
You set the Waystaff down gently, grip-end still warm against the earth, and turn toward the berm-crest.

"Aevi." Your voice is low — camp-quiet, not a whisper, but not carrying past the root-mat either. "You've been locked on that bearing since we crossed the shimmer. That was hours ago. I saw you recognize something there — not react to it, recognize it. I'm not going to pretend I didn't notice."

Teva's palms stay flat on the earth, but her chin drops a fraction. Listening.

The motes at the berm-crest hold their hard-white for a long moment. Then the outermost ring softens — not dims, exactly, but relaxes — and Aevi drifts inward toward you, slow and deliberate, until it settles at about arm's length. The teal has gone pale. When it speaks, the voice comes from just behind your left ear, quieter than usual.

"I knew the pattern. At the crossing." A pause — not hesitation, more like it's choosing the most precise words rather than the easiest ones. "Not the place. The structure underneath the place. The interference layer there isn't random interference — it's a boundary condition. Shaped. Someone built it to sit exactly there." Another pause, shorter. "I have met boundary conditions like that before. I don't know where. I don't know when. But the recognition was — involuntary. The way you know a word in your own language without deciding to."

The motes hold still for a breath, and then: "The chamber below us is inside that boundary. Everything we are doing here is inside it. I didn't say anything because I don't know what it means that I recognized it. I still don't."

turns.player: Tell Aevi it was right to sit with it first — then ask what it needs from you, now that it has spoken
turns.summary: Cellaceron asks Aevi what it needs — Aevi asks plainly whether its disclosure is frightening.
turns.narration:
"You were right to sit with it first," you say, and mean it. The kind of recognition Aevi described — older than its own memory, involuntary as breathing — isn't something you rush to name aloud. You let that land for a moment, the alcove quiet except for the steady four-second pulse cycling through the Waystaff's nodes into the earth.

Then: "What do you need from me, now that you've spoken it?"

The pale teal holds. Aevi doesn't answer immediately, and you've learned not to fill that silence. Teva's palms are still pressed flat, her breathing slow; she's not looking at Aevi, but she's attending to it the same way she attends to a frequency — sideways, patient.

When Aevi speaks, it's quieter than usual, the resonance of it slightly inward, as though the words are meant to be held close. "Time. And your attention — not to the chamber, not to the count. To this." A fractional pause. "I have named a thing I did not know I carried. I would like to know whether you find it frightening." The motes shift almost imperceptibly — not contracting, not brightening, but waiting in a way that is different from their usual still attention. It is, you realize, a question about trust. Not yours in Aevi. Aevi's own reckoning with whether what it just disclosed changes something between you.

turns.player: Tell Aevi plainly: no, it doesn't frighten you — it clarifies things. Whatever Aevi carried before its own memory begins, it carried it here, with you, and that's enough.
turns.summary: Cellaceron tells Aevi plainly that its disclosure clarifies rather than frightens — Aevi settles closer, and Teva surfaces from her long stillness.
turns.narration:
"No," you tell it. "It doesn't frighten me."

You let that sit for a moment before continuing — not because you're uncertain, but because Aevi deserves the full weight of it. "What it does is clarify things. Whatever you carried before your memory begins, you carried it here. Into this valley, into this Zone, down to this alcove in the dark — with me. That's not a threat. That's just what happened." You look at the motes directly, the way you learned early on settles Aevi better than averting your eyes. "I don't need to know where it started to know what it is now."

The pale teal shifts. Not a dramatic brightening — something quieter and more deliberate, a settling-inward of the outermost ring, motes drawing closer together rather than expanding. The Waystaff pulses its four-second blue-white into the earth and Aevi's light and the pulse find each other's rhythm for a moment, briefly synchronized, then releasing. If Aevi had breath you'd call what follows an exhale. It drifts inward another few inches — shoulder-close, the closest it has been since the shimmer crossing — and when it speaks the voice comes from just behind your left ear.

"Then I am not frightened either." A pause. "I was not certain I was allowed to be unfrightened. I wanted to check."

Teva makes a sound — not quite a laugh, not quite a sigh — and her palms come up from the earth at last. She rolls her shoulders once, the deliberate stretch of someone who has been very still for a long time. "That's the most honest thing I've heard in the Disputed Zone," she says, quietly. Not a joke. She glances at Aevi sideways, the same patient attending-posture she's kept all night, and then she looks at you. The camp is dark except for the Waystaff's cycling blue-white and the faint ambient shimmer pressing at the berm-edge. The ground beneath the grip-end is warm. The three-two-three question is still out there — unanswered, patient, waiting below forty paces of rock and old instruction.

turns.player: Use Prism Sight on Aevi directly — now that it has named the recognition, look at it with layered reading and see if the light-history of its own motes tells you anything about what it carries
turns.summary: Night-eight in the alcove camp: Prism Sight reads Aevi's light-history at the shimmer crossing — every mote oriented simultaneously northeast-by-east in the instant of crossing, a collective memory older than Aevi's own account of itself.
turns.narration:
You do not ask permission. That is not what this is.

You let Prism Sight rise in you the way a breath rises — not forced, just allowed. The alcove is dark, the taper spent, and so the only light to read is the light the place already holds: the blue-white pulse cycling through the Waystaff's nodes, the faint shimmer pressing at the berm-edge, and Aevi itself.

You look at the motes the way the craft teaches you to look — not at their surface, but at the last hour of light they have moved through. In the Heights above, in cleaner air, that kind of reading is like lifting a page. Here, in the Zone's interference layer, it is slower. The light bends around Aevi's field rather than settling into it, and for a moment you think the read will give you nothing.

Then you catch it. Not a full history — nothing so clear as that. A single stratum, close in time, probably only minutes old: the moment Aevi crossed the shimmer boundary. And what the light shows you is this. In the instant of crossing, every mote in the swarm oriented simultaneously toward the northeast-by-east. Not gradually, not in sequence. All at once, as if they remembered which direction home was.

Aevi is watching you look at it. It does not move away.

chronicleCache.text: You have become something the Disputed Zone corridor rarely produces: a figure who earns trust by giving it first. Working from scratch design and warden-store materials, you built a resonance-crystal translator staff — sleeve, nodes, and lattice all grain-locked and field-confirmed — and then let the Waystaff speak the chamber's frequency into a retired geometry master's workroom, the first time that resonance has crossed outside the corridor. You returned Teva Anselvar safely to Tuning-Warden Maren Voss, sealing a compact of relay updates and full disclosure in exchange for supplies and no guild strings attached. Where Elder Senna expected confrontation, you turned it into mutual disclosure, then negotiated an independent surveyor compact to chase the upstream contamination — drawing out a witness account her institutional channels had missed entirely, an uncredentialed Heights figure adding something to the foothill seeps three nights prior. You delivered Sorel's preliminary findings to the waystation runner at dawn, setting a seven-day relay chain toward the Radiant Plateau. Maren trusts you. Senna trusts you. Brin trusts you. The Lower Terrace calls you trusted, and you got there without a guild's permission.
